subject: Geography
title: The Korean DMZ
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Korean DMZ.

hook.text: The Korean DMZ is 4 km wide, 250 km long, and the most heavily mined border on Earth.

buildup.text: Since 1953, it has separated North and South Korea with fences, guards, and landmines.

discovery.text: Because no humans enter, the DMZ has become an accidental wildlife sanctuary.

twist.text: Rare cranes, leopards, and bears thrive in the strip between two hostile armies.

climax.text: The world's most dangerous border accidentally created one of Asia's last wild places.

punchline.text: Where soldiers can't go, nature moves in.

quiz.question: What unexpected role has the Korean DMZ played?
options: It became an accidental wildlife sanctuary
